updates.tweet_text:
Yesterday was charting 101 in #ES_F: We lost the 7390 shelf to trigger down. Support was 7247. Held *exact*. Targets were 7358, 7390 to backtest the shelf. We hit 7390, sold

Plan today: CPI 830am. 7311=support (held). 7327 reclaim sees 7345, 7358, 7390. 7311 fails dip 7284, 7247 https://t.co/VUDrGTZwGq
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7390, 7247, 7358, 7311, 7327, 7345, 7284) are already in the morning plan. Reaffirmations only, no new levels.

Another day of pinpoint precision in #ES_F and we won't get cleaner action. The long trigger was given live: The 7326 reclaim prior to CPI. The targets were given live: 7345 (hit), 7358 (hit), 7390 (hit literally exact). That's all for today

updates.tweet_text: Note: Remember today's target (7390) was also yesterday's. 7390 remains bull/bear line being the backtest of yesterday's breakdown. ES follows a standard 3 step process: Shelf fails (7390) which causes elevator down. Failed Breakdown, we long. Backtest shot. Reclaim bottoms
updates.notes: 7390 is already in the morning plan. Tweet is process commentary/reaffirmation of existing level, no new actionable prices introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @GlobalFX365 Thats the point of them!

updates.tweet_text: @Jbuehler777 Its just a major backtest point. These are notoriously trappy because they serve as gateways. I don't trade these personally, but ocne the market is done trapping everyone we can trend very hard
updates.notes: Pure educational commentary about backtest behavior; no specific price named or actionable level introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @EStrader33 @PermaFader I take those on occasion if its a very clear S/R shelf but I've been reducing those as well. For me if I just take Failed Breakdowns, its very very hard for me to having a losing trade, something would have to go extremely wrong or a big headline hit right after I get in

updates.tweet_text: @EStrader33 @PermaFader Reclaims, backtest longs, and shorts though win rates are far lower. For shorts the win rate is not even 50%

updates.tweet_text:
Pure chop after this mornings CPI squeeze. 7327 reclaim was the 8am long trigger given in #ES_F. My final target was 7390 (bull/bear line). We hit 90, sold

Next setup was the trap of the 7308 low, given 11:14AM. At 11:52AM, we swept it to 05, recovered. 7358, 7390 remain upsides https://t.co/8ctNnVfae5
updates.notes: All prices mentioned (7327, 7390, 7308, 7358) are morning-plan levels or hindsight trade recaps. No new actionable levels introduced.

updates.tweet_text: @Shimsham24 The low is there, but that low is part of a shelf. There is a mini IHS shelf there with the left and right shoulder being at 7308, so I use 7308
updates.notes: 7308 is a reply explaining a technical shelf observation to a user, not an explicit actionable level with naming language directed at followers.

updates.tweet_text: @OnTheHooch Relative to the size of the rally though this is still very shallow. It will continue though until we recover a breakdown shelf (like 7390 or 7527).
updates.notes: 7390 and 7527 are both morning-plan levels; tweet is observational commentary on pullback depth, no new actionable levels named.

updates.tweet_text: @OnTheHooch 27 is still a big magnet. Once we recover 7390 it would be the next target up
updates.notes: 7327 and 7390 are both morning-plan levels; tweet is a reaffirmation/observation of existing levels, not a new actionable call.

updates.tweet_text:
Chop all day after this mornings rip to the 7390 backtest/final target given in #ES_F. Bears control below, bulls above. Very simple

7308 remains a level we keep trapping. We trapped it once, rallied to 7340s. Same trigger applies with 7327, 7345, 7358 above. 7284, 7247 below https://t.co/2biNzfEyXl

updates.tweet_text: @bmf39 It depends on what price is doing. If there is a trigger that is close and Im concerned it could trigger before 4pm I'll send it a little early

updates.tweet_text: Closing update #ES_F: Very choppy session after a clean start to the day where we rallied to 7390+ target, rejected, and headed down. 7308 must recover to see another squeeze. Downsides remain 7284, 7247. Newsletter was just sent with specific entries from here
updates.notes: 7308 is not in morning plan but used as a condition ('must recover'), not explicitly named as a new actionable level with naming language. 7284/7247 are morning plan reaffirmations.

updates.tweet_text: @ES_Money_Print @bmf39 If there's a nearby major trigger, I don't want it to trigger then I release the newsletter at 4pm and there is nothing to write about, so if we get let late day volatility I try to release it a little early
updates.notes: No price levels mentioned at all; purely conversational explanation of newsletter timing process.
